decisions.decision: Research-only for now. No code changes.
decisions.rationale: 5 active epics already in flight. Ideas catalogued for future prioritization.

decisions.decision: Deprioritized — skills already selectively loaded via /spawn and /review mappings.
decisions.rationale: Initial hypothesis that skills were bulk-loaded was incorrect. Existing pattern is adequate.

decisions.decision: Low-effort candidate when agents misfire. Add 2-3 exemplar runs to bridge.py or agent prompt.
decisions.rationale: PTC research showed input_examples improved accuracy 72%->90%. Same principle applies to agent task descriptions.

decisions.decision: Marginal benefit. Claude Code agents call Bash sequentially regardless.
decisions.rationale: Compound verify script saves round-trips only if the runtime supports parallel execution, which Claude Code does not for sequential tool calls.

decisions.decision: Strategic but deferred. Needs own research artifact if pursued.
decisions.rationale: stdlib urllib wrapper enables CI/cron execution without Claude Code. Large scope, conflicts with stdlib-only constraint if anthropic SDK is desired.

constraints: Python stdlib only — anthropic SDK requires httpx/pydantic
5 active epics — no bandwidth for new code work
MCP tools cannot be called via PTC yet — limits optimization potential
openQuestions: Will Claude Code Agent Teams leverage PTC internally for multi-tool agent turns?
When will MCP tools become PTC-callable?
Are agents misfiring on TaskDescV2 prompts frequently enough to justify examples?
Should headless mode be a separate feature or part of a larger API integration story?
